what_it_does: Implements the Switch as a function component using React.createElement (h). Renders a single button with type='button', role='switch', aria-checked reflecting the checked prop, and class cv-switch. On click (only when onChange is provided) it calls onChange(!checked, e), emitting the toggled value. The caller's className is merged and ...rest is spread onto the button.
observations: Renders only a button element (the track/thumb visuals come entirely from .cv-switch CSS) — no children
onClick attached only when onChange is given; emits !checked (the next state) plus the event
Uses global React with const h = React.createElement (no JSX)
Controlled component: no internal state, relies on checked from props
standouts: Entire on/off appearance (including the gold track) is delegated to the .cv-switch token class; the component is purely a controlled toggle shell
conventions: Token-class wrapper with no inline styles or literals (one-home design discipline)
notes: A token-class-only controlled switch; clicking emits the negated checked value, and all visuals (gold track when on) live in .cv-switch CSS.
purpose_vs_actual: Match — implements the on/off toggle the header comment and .d.ts describe.
intention_signals: To express an on/off switch as a minimal accessible button shell over design-system CSS, so the track/thumb/gold-on look lives in tokens and the JS only flips and reports the boolean.
novelty: Buildless React.createElement authoring of a pure controlled toggle with no internal state.
contribution: Contributes the structural/ARIA implementation of the on/off switch as a controlled button that emits the negated value, delegated entirely to the .cv-switch token class.
summary_for_embedding: React.createElement implementation of an on/off Switch as a controlled button with role=switch, aria-checked, and an onChange emitting the negated checked value, styled via .cv-switch.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the buildless, token-class-only controlled on/off switch whose visuals live in design-system CSS.
